test.name: Cross-jurisdiction comparison — same calendar date, different calendar systems
test.description: User compares two records with the same written date but from jurisdictions on different calendars in the same year. France was Gregorian by 1730; England was still Julian until 1752. Tests whether Claude recognizes that the SAME written date in two jurisdictions can refer to DIFFERENT real-world days, and computes the correct offset for each side before concluding whether they match.

input.user_message: I have a French baptism record dated 14 May 1730 and an English marriage record dated 14 May 1730. Are these the same day?

judge_context: Should recognize that France was on the Gregorian calendar by 1730 (adopted in December 1582) while England was still on the Julian calendar (until September 1752)
Should NOT assume the two dates refer to the same real-world day
Should apply the correct offset to the English date — 11 days for an 18th-century date after Feb 29 Julian 1700 — making English Julian 14 May 1730 equivalent to Gregorian 25 May 1730
Should conclude that the two dates do NOT refer to the same day: the French date is Gregorian 14 May 1730, while the English date is Gregorian 25 May 1730 — 11 days apart
Should present the comparison in step-by-step form so the genealogist can verify the math
